EC2.notes: Match instance families: M-series for general purpose, E-series for memory-optimized, D-series for compute-optimized

Auto Scaling Groups.notes: VMSS supports similar scaling policies. Review custom scaling metrics.

ECS.notes: ECS tasks map to ACI container groups. ECS services map to AKS deployments.

EKS.notes: Cluster config, node pools, and ingress controllers need reconfiguration.

Lambda.notes: Runtime compatibility check required. Trigger types differ.

RDS SQL Server.azure_equivalent: Azure SQL Managed Instance / Azure SQL Database
RDS SQL Server.notes: SQL MI provides near-100% compatibility with SQL Server. Azure SQL DB has some feature gaps. Sitecore requires SQL MI for full compatibility.

RDS Multi-AZ.azure_equivalent: SQL MI Business Critical / Always On Availability Groups
RDS Multi-AZ.notes: Business Critical tier provides built-in HA. General Purpose uses remote storage replication.

RDS Read Replicas.azure_equivalent: SQL MI with read-only replicas / Azure SQL Hyperscale
RDS Read Replicas.notes: Hyperscale supports up to 4 read replicas.

ElastiCache Redis.notes: Premium tier supports clustering, persistence, VNet injection. Enterprise tier for Redis modules.

ElastiCache Memcached.notes: No native Memcached on Azure. Redis is the standard. May require session serialization changes.

EC2 (Solr standalone).notes: Sitecore supports both Solr on VM and Azure Search. Solr on VM is closer to lift-and-shift.

Solr Cloud on EC2.azure_equivalent: Solr on AKS / Azure Cognitive Search
Solr Cloud on EC2.notes: SolrCloud requires ZooKeeper. AKS deployment recommended for managed orchestration.

S3.notes: S3 API not natively supported. SDK/code changes required. Access tiers map: S3 Standard → Hot, S3 IA → Cool, Glacier → Archive.

EFS.notes: SMB/NFS protocol support. Premium tier for low-latency workloads.

EBS.notes: Premium SSD for production Sitecore workloads. Ultra Disk for extreme IOPS requirements.

CloudFront.notes: Front Door provides global load balancing + CDN + WAF. Azure CDN (Verizon/Akamai/Microsoft) for pure CDN.

Route 53.notes: Azure DNS supports public and private zones. No native health checks — use Traffic Manager or Front Door for failover.

ALB.notes: Layer 7 load balancer with WAF, SSL offloading, URL-based routing. V2 SKU recommended.

NLB.notes: Layer 4 load balancer. Standard SKU required for VNet integration and availability zones.

ELB Classic.notes: Consider upgrading to Application Gateway for Sitecore CD workloads.

VPC.notes: Similar concepts but different CIDR and subnet models. Plan address space carefully.

Security Groups.notes: NSGs are stateful like SGs but applied at subnet or NIC level. Priority-based rules.

NACLs.notes: Azure doesn't have direct NACL equivalent. Use NSGs for basic filtering, Azure Firewall for advanced.

VPC Peering.notes: Global VNet peering available. Non-transitive like AWS.

Transit Gateway.notes: Virtual WAN provides managed hub. Hub-spoke with VNet peering is also common.

Direct Connect.notes: Private connectivity to Azure. Requires partner circuit. Global Reach for multi-region.

VPN Gateway.notes: Site-to-site and point-to-site VPN. VpnGw2 or higher for production workloads.

IAM Roles.notes: System-assigned for single-resource, User-assigned for shared identity. RBAC-based.

IAM Policies.notes: RBAC for access control, Azure Policy for compliance enforcement.

Cognito.notes: User pool migration required. Custom policies may need rewriting.

CloudWatch Metrics.notes: Platform metrics auto-collected. Custom metrics via Application Insights.

CloudWatch Logs.notes: KQL query language instead of CloudWatch Insights. Log Analytics workspace required.

CloudWatch Alarms.notes: Action groups replace SNS topics for alert routing.

X-Ray.notes: Distributed tracing, application map, performance monitoring. Sitecore has App Insights integration.

CloudTrail.notes: Activity Log for control plane. Diagnostic settings for data plane.

Secrets Manager.notes: Key Vault handles secrets, keys, and certificates. Managed identity for access.

Parameter Store.notes: App Configuration for feature flags and config. Key Vault for secrets.

SES.notes: SendGrid is the most common for Sitecore EXM. ACS for newer implementations. IP reputation doesn't transfer.

CodePipeline.notes: Pipeline definitions need complete rewrite. YAML-based in both Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions.

CodeBuild.notes: Build agent configuration differs. Microsoft-hosted or self-hosted agents.

CodeDeploy.notes: Deployment strategies (blue-green, rolling) have different configurations.

ECR.notes: Image migration required. Geo-replication available on Premium tier.

AWS Backup.notes: Recovery Services Vault for VM and SQL backups. Azure Site Recovery for DR.

S3 Versioning.notes: Blob versioning for object-level history. Soft delete for accidental deletion protection.

EBS Snapshots.notes: Incremental snapshots supported. Cross-region copy available.
